sanskrit:
समं कायशिरोग्रीवं धारयन्नचलं स्थिरः।
संप्रेक्ष्य नासिकाग्रं स्वं दिशश्चानवलोकयन्॥
प्रशान्तात्मा विगतभीर्ब्रह्मचारिव्रते स्थितः।
मनः संयम्य मच्चित्तो युक्त आसीत मत्परः॥
transliteration:
samaṃ kāya-śiro-grīvaṃ dhārayann acalaṃ sthiraḥ |
samprekṣya nāsikāgraṃ svaṃ diśaś cānavalokayan ||
praśāntātmā vigata-bhīr brahmacāri-vrate sthitaḥ |
manaḥ saṃyamya mat-citto yukta āsīta mat-paraḥ ||
literal_meaning: Holding body-head-neck straight and still; gazing at the tip of the nose without looking around; mind tranquil, fear gone, established in the vow of celibacy; subduing the mind, consciousness fixed on Me, the yogī sits making Me the supreme goal.
contextual_meaning: Krishna prescribes the somatic posture and ethical foundation that stabilize attention for deep meditation: physical alignment, sensory withdrawal, emotional safety, and celibate energy conservation converge into a single-pointed love-directed awareness of the indwelling Divine.
psychological_state: Parasympathetic dominance; low-noise internal milieu conducive to flow states and self-transcendence.
trigger_scenario: A professional prepares for a high-stakes presentation by sitting upright, softening the gaze, breathing evenly, and silently dedicating the outcome to something larger than personal success.
anchor_text: Sit steady, gaze softly, breathe calm, and rest your mind in quiet love for the Divine within.
